# MBD-Framework — YellowHapax

> 18 standalone simulation labs implementing the Memory as Baseline Deviation (MBD) framework across six published papers. Every lab is a runnable Python module. No frontend required. The full loop: read theory at the Noetic Lab → run labs here → the Fermi Solution (Paper 9) closes the arc.

## Core Equation

B(t+1) = B(t)·(1−λ) + I(t)·λ

An agent's personality is not a label — it is a vector in state space. Every significant experience shifts that vector. λ is how deeply the event rewrites who you are. Every lab in this repository explores a consequence of this single equation.

## Repository

- Source: https://github.com/YellowHapax/MBD-Framework
- DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18652919
- License: Apache 2.0
- Author ORCID: 0009-0007-6676-4897

## Quick Start

```bash
pip install -r requirements.txt
python -m labs.paper1_baseline.eq_lab
python -m labs.paper4_coupling.phenomena_bpd
python -m labs.paper6_resonant_gate.zeta_lab
```

Each lab module exposes three functions: `describe()` → metadata dict, `run(**kwargs)` → simulation results dict, `plot(results)` → Matplotlib figure.

## Paper 1: Memory as Baseline Deviation

DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.17381536 — 6 labs

- `labs/paper1_baseline/eq_lab`: Two-agent convergence under the core B(t+1) equation. Baseline starting points, λ values, and how quickly two agents converge or diverge.

- `labs/paper1_baseline/phenomena_adhd`: Typical vs ADHD plasticity — λ = 0.1 (stable, slow-updating) vs λ = 0.8 with ×0.85 decay coefficient. The attentional cost of high plasticity.

- `labs/paper1_baseline/phenomena_ossification`: Plasticity λ(t) decaying exponentially over time. Why change gets harder as a mathematical prediction, not a moral judgment.

- `labs/paper1_baseline/phenomena_sbs`: Chronic error driving λ increase → eventual plasticity collapse. The Shaken Baby Syndrome model — trauma as runaway λ.

- `labs/paper1_baseline/phenomena_phantom`: Constant baseline vs null input — phantom limb as MBD prediction. What happens to the state vector when sensory input is severed.

- `labs/paper1_baseline/phenomena_bipolar`: Piecewise manic-euthymic-depressive baseline with asymmetric λ. Mood state as attractor basin; state-switching as λ-governed phase transition.

## Paper 2: In Pursuit of the Markov Tensor

DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.17537185 — 1 lab

- `labs/paper2_markov/echo_chamber`: Group κ convergence followed by external shock → catastrophic fragmentation. How coupling creates vulnerability: high κ makes agreement fast and fragmentation total.

## Paper 3: Episodic Recall as Resonant Re-instantiation

DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.17374270 — 1 lab

- `labs/paper3_episodic/reinstantiation`: Novelty-gated episodic encoding (sigmoid gate at novelty threshold θ_h = 0.35), context-scaffolded recall. Memory as re-instantiation, not retrieval.

## Paper 4: The Coupling Asymmetry

DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18519187 — 5 labs

- `labs/paper4_coupling/phenomena_aspd`: κ locked near 0 — immune to relational constitution. ASPD model: what it looks like mathematically when an agent cannot be changed by others.

- `labs/paper4_coupling/phenomena_bpd`: κ oscillating wildly between idealisation and devaluation. BPD model: the attractor basin is unstable; small perturbations flip coupling sign.

- `labs/paper4_coupling/phenomena_asymmetry`: One-way coupling. Host κ = 0.9 (highly open), Parasite κ = 0.1 (effectively closed). The Host changes; the Parasite does not. Asymmetry is the mechanism of exploitation.

- `labs/paper4_coupling/phenomena_echo`: Internalised past Other — D_self = 0.8, κ_self = 0.7. The introjected voice as a coupling term that persists after the external agent is gone.

- `labs/paper4_coupling/phenomena_fragmentation`: Multiple baselines active, state-dependent encoding and recall. DID model: fragmentation as an adaptive response to irreconcilable baseline demands.

## Paper 5: The Emergent Gate

DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.17344091 — 2 labs

- `labs/paper5_emergent_gate/mood_incongruent`: Sad baseline plus a happy event produces enormous novelty signal (high B(t) − I(t) gap). P6 prediction: mood-incongruent encoding is louder, not quieter.

- `labs/paper5_emergent_gate/dual_resonance`: Resonance amplification vs overshadowing at multiple κ values. How coupling strength determines whether two signals reinforce or cancel.

## Paper 6: The Resonant Gate

DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.17352481 — 3 labs

- `labs/paper6_resonant_gate/resonant_gate`: Two agents. κ grows over time. Identical external insight arrives at low-κ vs high-κ moments. With low κ the insight bounces off; with high κ it propagates and rewrites both baselines. The Gate is not the insight — it is the coupling.

- `labs/paper6_resonant_gate/zeta_lab`: Comparative trials at ζ = 0.1, 0.5, 0.9. ζ is the self-preservation floor — the minimum fraction of the current baseline retained regardless of λ. Tests the tradeoff between openness and identity continuity.

- `labs/paper6_resonant_gate/deontological_tests`: P8 (moral blindness at κ = 0), P9 (error dissociation — acting on a cached baseline that no longer matches state), P10 (moral immunity — baseline ossified against update by ethical input). Three failure modes of the Resonant Gate.

## Analysis Modules

- `analysis/trauma_model.py`: Core MBD equations — B(t+1) = B(t)(1−λ) + I(t)λ and κ dynamics. Canonical implementation.

## Dynamics Engine

- `dynamics/influence_cube.py`: Stella octangula vertex geometry — InfluenceState and CubeLambdas. The 3D social influence space with Haven and Sink tetrahedra interpenetrating. Axes: Locus (Internal→External), Coupling (Low→High), Temporality (Static→Dynamic).

- `dynamics/field_agent.py`: FieldAgent with attractor basins, behavioral exemplars, and a novelty field. The dynamics-layer agent model — not a simple scalar but a full attractor-basin creature.

- `dynamics/social_fabric.py`: Group interaction probability under κ-coupling pressure. The population-level consequence of individual coupling dynamics.

## Notebooks

- `notebooks/01_baseline_deviation.ipynb`: Paper 1 walkthrough — interactive derivation of the core equation and all six baseline phenomena.

- `notebooks/04_executive_load.ipynb`: Paper 4 walkthrough — coupling asymmetry with interactive κ sliders.

## Web UI (Optional)

```bash
cd lab && npm install && npm run build && cd ..
python lab/server.py   # → http://localhost:8050
```

FastAPI backend + React/Recharts frontend. Pre-built `lab/dist/` is included in the repo — the `npm` steps may be skippable.

## Citation

If you use this work, please cite the relevant paper. Machine-readable metadata: `CITATION.cff`.

Primary citation:
> Everett, B. (2025). Memory as Baseline Deviation: A Formal Framework for Personality as State-Space Dynamics. Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.17381536

Full paper DOI table:

| # | Title | DOI |
|---|-------|-----|
| 1 | Memory as Baseline Deviation | 10.5281/zenodo.17381536 |
| 2 | In Pursuit of the Markov Tensor | 10.5281/zenodo.17537185 |
| 3 | Episodic Recall as Resonant Re-instantiation | 10.5281/zenodo.17374270 |
| 4 | The Coupling Asymmetry | 10.5281/zenodo.18519187 |
| 5 | The Emergent Gate | 10.5281/zenodo.17344091 |
| 6 | The Resonant Gate | 10.5281/zenodo.17352481 |

## Theory Library

The theoretical papers that this code implements live at the Noetic Lab:

- Site: https://yellowhapax.github.io/Noetic-Lab/
- Machine-readable index: https://yellowhapax.github.io/Noetic-Lab/llms.txt
- Structured manifest: https://yellowhapax.github.io/Noetic-Lab/content.json

The traversal order: read the theory papers at the Noetic Lab → run the labs here → the Fermi Solution (Paper 9, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.18879430) closes the arc from individual cognition to civilizational filter.
